title: OpenPlanTrace Scan Result
description: Schema for the schema-versioned JSON emitted by OpenPlanTrace scan exports. The contract is strict about stable top-level fields and core evidence shapes, while allowing additional detector-specific fields for forward-compatible evolution.

unit.description: Logical drawing unit used by all exported page geometry.

sourceKind.description: How this object candidate was introduced by the deterministic scanner.

sourceWallComponentId.description: Wall graph component ID when sourceKind is WallComponentIsland; null otherwise.

sourceWallComponentKind.description: Wall graph component kind when sourceKind is WallComponentIsland; null otherwise.

detectedTag.description: Normalized deterministic industrial/equipment tag code recognized for this object candidate, when present.

detectedTagSourcePrimitiveId.description: Source primitive ID for the text or symbol evidence that supplied detectedTag.

detectedTags.description: Distinct deterministic tag codes recognized across candidate occurrences in this object group.

childObjectIds.description: Raw object candidate IDs that make up this compound object. These detections are retained in objects and can still be reviewed.

objectGroupIds.description: Repeated-symbol group IDs that intersect this aggregate, when any.

composition.description: Machine-readable child-object makeup for this aggregate, preserving category/source distributions and per-child bounds so consumers can audit why multiple detections were collapsed into one physical or semantic object.

routingInfluence.description: How route/loop/layout consumers should treat this aggregate instead of separately consuming each child object.

suppressChildObjectsForRouting.description: When true, route/loop consumers should prefer this aggregate over the listed child objects to avoid counting internal symbol parts as separate obstacles.

suppressedObjects.description: Detailed per-object routing suppression records. These explain why a raw object candidate should not be independently consumed and which aggregate obstacle or room-use hint replaces it.

ignoredObjects.description: Detailed object candidates intentionally omitted from routing obstacles, including reason, bounds, source evidence, and optional suppression or room-use links.

suppressedObjectCandidateIds.description: Object candidate IDs that should not be independently consumed by route/loop/layout tools because a higher-level aggregate already represents them.

ignoredObjectCandidateIds.description: Object candidate IDs that are intentionally not exported as routing obstacles.

topologySpans.description: Trusted wall graph spans derived from graph nodes. These are the trimmed/split topology segments consumers should prefer over raw wall centerlines when available.

readyForCoordinatePlacement.description: True only when the passage's opening placement is internally coherent enough for exact downstream coordinates.

requiresReview.description: True when opening placement, operation, confidence, or related routing evidence requires human review before exact downstream use.

suppressedByAggregateId.description: Object aggregate that replaces or explains this raw child object for routing/loop/layout consumers.

action.description: Recommended downstream action for route/loop/layout consumers.

replacementRoutingObstacleId.description: Routing obstacle ID to consume instead of the child object, when the aggregate is an obstacle.

roomUseHintId.description: Room-use hint ID to consume instead of the child object, when the aggregate is semantic evidence only.

reason.description: Why this object candidate is not exported as a routing obstacle.

hostWallId.description: Primary wall identifier used as the placement anchor when one is available.

anchorWallIds.description: Wall segment ids used to derive the placement reference line. Split-wall openings can include both adjacent wall fragments.

referenceLine.description: Page-space line used as the local placement axis for offsets and direction vectors.

referenceLineMillimeters.description: Metric version of referenceLine when calibration is reliable.

footprintBounds.description: Axis-aligned page-space bounds of the opening footprint across the wall-normal depth.

footprintCorners.description: Four page-space footprint corners ordered start-minus-normal, end-minus-normal, end-plus-normal, start-plus-normal.

startJambLine.description: Wall-normal jamb line at the opening start point.

endJambLine.description: Wall-normal jamb line at the opening end point.

depthDrawingUnits.description: Wall-normal footprint depth in drawing units.

hostWallStartParameter.description: Opening start parameter on referenceLine where 0 is referenceLine.start and 1 is referenceLine.end.

side.description: Room side relative to the opening normal vector. PositiveNormalSide and NegativeNormalSide give deterministic side-of-opening placement context.

roomSidePoint.description: Page-space sample point on the room side of the opening, useful for downstream room-to-opening placement checks.

nearestBoundaryPoint.description: Nearest detected room-boundary point to the opening midpoint.

signedDistanceFromOpening.description: Signed page-space offset from opening midpoint to the room center along the opening normal vector.

sourceWallStartOffsetDrawingUnits.description: Projected span start offset along the source wall centerline in drawing units.

sourceWallEndOffsetDrawingUnits.description: Projected span end offset along the source wall centerline in drawing units.

sourceWallProjectedLengthDrawingUnits.description: Absolute difference between projected source-wall start/end offsets.

sourceWallStartProjectionDistanceDrawing.description: Distance from topology span start to the source wall centerline projection.

sourceWallEndProjectionDistanceDrawingUn.description: Distance from topology span end to the source wall centerline projection.
